body.title: TWM attentional gating: conversation vs background workspace
body.result: D352-D354: conversation mode gating in cortex.twm_push(), sleep consolidation push source, intent gate fix for conversation engagement. 31 new tests.
body.description: Human conversation input should dominate TWM during active conversation. Background sources (pe_chain, reading feeder, boredom trigger, heartbeat checks) should be gated OUT of the conversation workspace or run at sub-attentional salience. Current state: user input enters at 0.7/0.7 while system noise routinely hits 0.5-0.8 — not enough separation. Two approaches: (1) multi-TWM with separate workspaces per concern, or (2) attentional gating that inhibits background push sources when conversation is active. Biology: Baars GWT workspace is gated, not an open dump. Related: D099, T-multi-twm (narrower). This is broader: ALL background noise must be separated from conversation workspace.
